preamble: Epenthesis of [d], also known as excrescence, typically occurs between a nasal or liquid and a following sound, creating a transitional stop.

examples.note: OE → ModE: excrescent [d] inserted between nasal [n] and rhotic [r].
OE → ModE: excrescent [d] inserted in the [nl] cluster.
Excrescent [d] inserted between word-final [r] and comparative suffix [-ər]; e.g. duur → duurder.

sources: Crystal, D. (2008). A Dictionary of Linguistics and Phonetics (6th ed.). Blackwell Publishing.
